name: Import / Export Documentation Checklist
shortBenefit: Verify all trade documents before shipment to avoid customs delays
intro: Use this comprehensive checklist to ensure every import or export shipment is backed by the correct documents — preventing costly delays at customs.

seo.title: Import Export Documentation Checklist India – Trade Docs Guide
seo.description: Complete checklist of import and export documents required in India. Commercial invoice, packing list, BL, LC, COO, and regulatory certificates.

items.text: Commercial Invoice (with HS code, unit price, total value, INCOTERM)
Packing List (carton-wise weight, dimensions, content)
Bill of Lading / Airway Bill (original or telex release)
Purchase Order / Sales Contract
Letter of Credit — confirmed and operative (if LC terms)
Certificate of Origin — notarised and legalised if required
Shipping Bill (export) / Bill of Entry (import)
IEC (Import Export Code) — active and valid
GST Registration for imports under IGST credit scheme
AD Code registered at port for export realization
Phytosanitary Certificate (for agricultural / food products)
Fumigation Certificate if required by importing country
Quality Inspection Certificate / Pre-shipment Inspection Report
BIS / FSSAI / Drug licence for regulated products
MSDS for chemicals / hazardous goods
Marine Insurance Certificate / Open Cover Policy
Bank Realisation Certificate (BRC) for DGFT benefits
FIRC (Foreign Inward Remittance Certificate) for advance payment
SWIFT copy of payment for TT advance cases
GR Form (Export Declaration Form) — RBI requirement

assumptions: Requirements vary by product and importing country. Always verify with your CHA.
disclaimer: This tool provides an indicative estimate for general planning only and is not professional, legal, financial, medical or engineering advice. Reference values are editable defaults that vary by location, vendor, regulation and date. Verify critical figures with a qualified professional. Varada Nexus accepts no liability for decisions made using this tool.

faq.q: What is an IEC code and who needs it?
faq.a: Importer Exporter Code is a 10-digit number from DGFT, mandatory for any business importing or exporting goods from India. One-time registration.

faq.q: What is a Certificate of Origin?
faq.a: A COO certifies the country where goods were produced. Required for customs clearance, FTA preferential duty claims, and importing country compliance.

faq.q: What happens if documents are missing at customs?
faq.a: Missing documents lead to demurrage, customs hold and potential auction of goods. Having all documents ready prevents costly delays.
